description: Demo of the procedural quest generator (server/src/quest/). A tiny self-contained Bureau contract desk: on entry it pays out any just-completed contract, then rolls a fresh TYPED quest (bounty / hunt / rescue / retrieve / investigate / two-stage strike), scaled to the player's level. Press TO MISSION (top bar) to play the generated encounter(s); LEAVE MISSION returns here, which rolls a different type. Multi-stage quests surface a TO MISSION 'next stage' button between their encounters. Quest steps grant XP during play; the contract pays coin on return.
customIntroduction: A bare contract desk — a board of slips, a money-box, and the quiet of a room that exists only to hand out work. A slip is already waiting on the board.
customContext: DEMO — QUEST GENERATOR. This is a contract hub. The board always has a freshly-rolled, procedurally-generated quest. The player presses TO MISSION (top bar) to play it and LEAVE MISSION to return for a new, different-typed one. Do not invent specific contract details — the engine sets the objective when the quest starts.
objective: Press TO MISSION (top bar) to play a procedurally-generated quest. Return here for a new one of a different type.

then.message: Last contract paid out. A fresh slip hits the board.
A Bureau contract is on the board — press TO MISSION (top bar) to play it. Come back here afterward for a different type of quest.
